rationale: The citing paper explicitly uses Hsee et al. (2015) to define lay rationalism and to argue that reason-versus-feelings orientation captures individual differences in need for justification. It then operationalizes this construct with the six-item Lay Rationalism Scale in Study 4 and tests predicted moderation of the upgrade effect by lay rationalism. Because the cited work is used to build hypotheses and provide a key measurement tool for mechanism testing, the citation plays a clear functional role beyond background listing.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the lay rationalism construct and scale from Hsee et al. (2015) to a new domain: upgrade-driven carelessness toward owned products. Specifically, it uses lay rationalism as a moderator to test when justification concerns intensify product neglect.
